source_section.title_label_raw: Built Form and Siting Requirements within the HR-2 and HR-1 Zones Applicability

sections.title_label_raw: Siting Requirements within the HR-2 and HR-1 Zones Applicability

provisions.heading_context_raw: Siting Requirements within the HR-2 and HR-1 Zones Applicability
provisions.text: With the exception of main buildings within a heritage conservation district, any main building erected, constructed, reconstructed, altered, or located, or an addition to any main building, within an HR- 2 or HR-1 zone shall meet the built form and siting requirements of this Chapter. Maximum Building Height

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall
provisions.text: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall not exceed the maximum required building height specified on Schedule 15. Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 197(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 197(2), any portion of a main building, either above or below grade, shall have a minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18. (RCCC- Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 197(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: If a minimum required front or flanking setback has not been specified on Schedule 18, the minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 1.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) Side Setback Requirements

provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum side setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any side yard.

provisions.text: For any high-rise building, any portion of the main building above the streetwall height shall have a minimum required setback of 12.5 metres from any side lot line.

provisions.text: There is no maximum required side setback. Rear Setback Requirements

provisions.text: For any tall mid-rise building, any portion of the main building exceeding a height of 7 storeys shall have a minimum required setback of 4.5 metres from a rear lot line. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24)

provisions.text: For any high-rise building, any portion of the main building above the streetwall height shall have a minimum required setback of 12.5 metres from any rear lot line. Maximum Streetwall Heights

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 201(2) and 201(3), any main building's

provisions.text: Twenty percent or less of the entire streetwall width, to a maximum of 10.0 metres, may be reduced in height, providing the height is not reduced below 1 storey (Diagram 7). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 201(2) and 201(3), any main building's
provisions.text: The minimum streetwall height required in Subsection 201(1) may be reduced to 1 storey along a streetline or a transportation reserve, if the slope exceeds 4% across the building width or building depth. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Streetwall Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building shall have a minimum required streetwall stepback of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: 2.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: 3.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: 4.5 metres for high-rise buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for any main building that meets Subclauses 200(1)(b)(i) or 200(1)(c)(i). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for a maximum of 20% of the length of any main building facing each streetline or a transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 202(2) and 202(3), any main building
provisions.text: No portion of any main building above the streetwall shall project beyond the streetwall towards the front or flanking lot line. Recessed Portions and Cantilevers within Streetwalls

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 203(2), the total combined width of all

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 203(2), the total combined width of all
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 203(2), the total combined width of all cantilevered and recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2.0 metres or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 203(2), the total combined width of all
provisions.text: Cantilevered and recessed portions described in Subsection 203(1) shall include portals, but exclude balconies and recessed pedestrian entrances. Maximum Lot Coverage

sections.title_label_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.

provisions.heading_context_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.
provisions.text: No maximum required lot coverage applies. Ground Floor Requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, a ground floor shall be established along any streetline or transportation reserve. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: On a corner lot, for any streetwall along a streetline or transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that exceeds a slope greater than 8%, extended across the entire width of the streetwall, the requirements for a ground floor do not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: A ground floor shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline or a transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 205(6), each ground floor required shall, for no less than 70% of each streetwall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 0.6 metre below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 205(5), commence no higher than 0.6 metre above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: In the event of a conflict between the coastal elevation requirements of Section 74 and Clause 205(4)(b), Clause 205(4)(b) shall not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor of a grade-related dwelling unit use shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 1.5 metres below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: commence no higher than 1.5 metres above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 205(8) and excluding a low-density dwelling use, a grade-related dwelling unit use, or a residential lobby on a floor containing a grade-related dwelling unit use, the minimum required ground floor height shall be 3.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor height required in Subsection 205(7) may be reduced where one of the following conditions applies:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: the ground floor height of an addition is equal to or greater than the ground floor height of the existing main building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 205(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: for a proposed building on a registered heritage property, the ground floor heights of abutting main buildings along the same streetline are such that the required ground floor height of the proposed main building would be inconsistent with the abutting main buildings. Grade-Oriented Premises

provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented premises shall:

provisions.text: excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, be located along any streetline; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E- Nov 11/22)

provisions.text: be required for the full width of the streetwall, excluding:

provisions.text: only one entrance to internal parking located above or below the ground floor, (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.text: a portal,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency exit, or

provisions.text: an entrance to a room dedicated to a solid waste management area; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: not exceed a maximum width of 24.0 metres for each premise facing a streetline: and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: contain an independent pedestrian entrance for each premise facing a streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subsection 206(1) shall not apply to:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a change of use;

provisions.text: a shared housing with special care use. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: For any addition to any main building, the requirement of Subsection 206(1) shall only apply to the length of the streetwall addition.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 206(2) and 206(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Grade-oriented premises shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline. Grade-Related Dwelling Units

sections.title_label_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of
provisions.text: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of one exterior pedestrian entrance. Side and Rear Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 208(2), for a tall mid-rise building, any

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 208(2), for a tall mid-rise building, any
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 208(2), for a tall mid-rise building, any portion of any main building above the height of the streetwall shall have a minimum required:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 208(2), for a tall mid-rise building, any
provisions.text: side stepback of 2.5 metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 208(2), for a tall mid-rise building, any
provisions.text: rear stepback of 4.5 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 208(2), for a tall mid-rise building, any
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 208(4), where a lot abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, the wall of any main building facing the lot with such zone shall have a required side or rear stepback, as measured from the lowest finished grade on that side of the main building, on the 2nd, 3rd, or 4th storey. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24) (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.text: 12.0 metres for high-rise buildings. Minimum Separation Distances

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main building is permitted on a lot in Subsection 89(3), a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between main buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Where more than one portion of a main building protrudes above grade, a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between the above grade portions.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 209(4), above the streetwall height, any portions of the same main building shall be separated by a minimum required distance of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 6.0 metres between a mid-rise typology and another mid- rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 6.0 metres between a mid-rise typology and a tall mid-rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a mid-rise typology and a high-rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a tall mid-rise typology and another tall mid-rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a tall mid-rise typology and a high- rise typology; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 25.0 metres between a high-rise typology and another high-rise typology. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Tower portions of two or more main buildings on the same lot shall be separated by a minimum required distance of 25.0 metres. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: For the property identified by PID 00092932, the minimum required separation distance between a tall mid-rise typology and another tall mid-rise typology shall be 10.0 metres (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22) Maximum Building Dimensions

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average finished grade, any main building shall not exceed the following maximum building dimension requirements:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: on a through lot:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building width of 64.0 metres, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building depth of 40.0 metres; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: on any other lot:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building width of 64.0 metres, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building depth of 64.0 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: The tower portion of any high-rise building shall not exceed the following maximum building dimension requirements of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building depth of 35.0 metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building width of 40.0 metres; and (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: an average floor area of 900 square metres per storey as calculated for the tower portion of the building. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: For the purpose of measuring building dimensions in Subsections 210(1) and 210(2), main buildings connected by a pedway shall be measured separately, excluding the pedway. Accessory Structures

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter
provisions.text: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter 19.
